name: Anthurium crystallinum
origin: Panama to Peru (humid tropical forest understory)
aboutText: Anthurium crystallinum is one of the foundational velvet anthuriums — a compact, self-supporting species from humid tropical forests spanning Panama to Peru, where it grows terrestrially or occasionally lithophytically on moist, organic-rich substrates in deep shade. The broad, cordate leaves are a deep velvety dark green with an open sinus and strikingly beautiful silvery-white primary venation that gives the plant its crystalline quality. Petioles arise from a short crown with surface roots, and the plant remains neatly self-supporting rather than climbing. As the parent of an enormous number of celebrated hybrids — crystallinum × magnificum, crystallinum × forgetii and many others — this is a species of genuine horticultural importance as well as great beauty in its own right.

morphology.venation: Striking silvery-white crystalline primary venation on deep velvety green blade
morphology.growthHabit: Short basal crown with surface roots, self-supporting
